name: Salesforce
one_liner: Leading enterprise CRM for sales, service, and marketing.
auth_note: OAuth2 (connected apps) is standard; JWT bearer and username-password flows also supported. Free Developer Edition org self-serve.
access_note: Free Developer Edition org signup gives full API access to create your own connected app and OAuth credentials.
mcp_note: Salesforce ships an official MCP server (salesforcecli/mcp, 60+ tools) as part of its CLI/DX toolchain.
main_blocker: None; free dev org plus OAuth2 connected apps, very broad REST/SOAP/GraphQL/Bulk surface.

name: HubSpot
one_liner: All-in-one CRM for marketing, sales, and service.
auth_note: OAuth2 for public/marketplace apps; private-app access tokens for single-account integrations. Legacy hapikey API keys deprecated. Free developer account available.
access_note: Free HubSpot account plus free developer account let you create apps and private-app tokens yourself.
mcp_note: HubSpot runs an official remote MCP server (developers.hubspot.com/ai-tools/mcp); currently read-leaning, community servers add writes.
main_blocker: None; free dev account, self-serve OAuth2/private-app tokens, broad REST surface.

name: Pipedrive
one_liner: Sales-pipeline CRM for tracking deals and follow-ups.
auth_note: Personal API token obtainable from account settings; OAuth2 for marketplace apps. Developer sandbox account available.
access_note: API token self-generated from Pipedrive settings; free developer sandbox and OpenAPI specs published.
mcp_note: No clearly official server; multiple community MCP servers exist (read-only and full-CRUD variants).
main_blocker: None; self-serve token, sandbox, broad REST API and official client libs.

name: Attio
one_liner: Modern, flexible CRM for teams and workflows.
auth_note: Workspace-scoped access tokens (Bearer) created self-serve under Settings > Developers; OAuth2 for multi-workspace published apps.
access_note: Free plan lets you create an integration and generate a scoped access token yourself.
mcp_note: Attio offers an official MCP server consolidating record/list/task/note operations; community servers also exist.
main_blocker: None; self-serve scoped access token on free plan, clean REST API.

name: Twenty
one_liner: Open-source, self-hostable CRM alternative to Salesforce.
auth_note: API keys / access tokens for REST and GraphQL; OAuth also supported. Being open-source you can self-host and mint your own tokens.
access_note: Open-source and self-hostable; cloud and local instances both let you generate API keys yourself for free.
mcp_note: No official Twenty MCP confirmed; community MCP servers wrap its GraphQL/REST API.
main_blocker: None; open-source, self-hostable, self-serve tokens, REST+GraphQL. Surface still maturing.

name: Podio
one_liner: Customizable work-management and CRM app platform.
auth_note: OAuth2; per-app API keys generated self-serve from account settings. Now owned/maintained by Citrix/Progress ShareFile.
access_note: Grab an API key per app from account settings; free/standard accounts can obtain credentials.
mcp_note: No official MCP server found; unclear community coverage for this legacy platform.
main_blocker: Legacy platform under Citrix with uncertain ongoing investment; API self-serve but ecosystem/maintenance risk.

name: Zoho CRM
one_liner: Cloud CRM within Zoho's broad business-app suite.
auth_note: OAuth2 only; register a self-client/server app in the Zoho API console to get client id/secret and tokens. Region-specific data centers.
access_note: Free Zoho CRM edition plus API console self-client registration yields OAuth credentials at no cost.
mcp_note: No confirmed official production MCP; community/third-party servers exist. Composio wraps Zoho as a toolkit.
main_blocker: OAuth2 self-client setup plus multi-region data-center routing add integration friction; otherwise obtainable free.

name: Close
one_liner: Sales CRM with built-in calling and email.
auth_note: API keys for scripts (self-generated in settings) or OAuth2 for user-facing integrations.
access_note: API key self-generated from Close settings; free trial available to obtain credentials.
mcp_note: Close ships an official MCP server (developer.close.com/mcp, help.close.com/docs/mcp-server).
main_blocker: None; self-serve API key, official MCP, broad REST API.

name: Copper
one_liner: CRM built for and integrated with Google Workspace.
auth_note: REST API uses API key plus user email headers; OAuth2 also documented for apps. No free tier (trial only), so a paid/trial account is needed to reach settings.
access_note: Copper has no free plan; API key is self-serve from settings but only within a trial or paid subscription.
mcp_note: No official MCP; several community/third-party servers (Apideck, Pipedream, n8n templates) exist.
main_blocker: No free tier: needs a paid (or time-limited trial) Copper account before the self-serve API key is reachable.

name: DealCloud
one_liner: Intapp deal/relationship CRM for private capital markets.
auth_note: OAuth2 client-credentials via admin-provisioned API keys / authorization tokens within a licensed Intapp DealCloud tenant.
access_note: Requires a paid enterprise Intapp DealCloud tenant; API keys are provisioned by a workspace admin, not self-serve signup.
mcp_note: DealCloud docs announce an official MCP server (launching July 2026); Python and C# SDKs also provided.
main_blocker: No self-serve access: needs a licensed enterprise DealCloud/Intapp tenant and admin-provisioned API credentials.
